name: labor_workplace_injury_spike_coincides_lumen_car_fair
premise: A Labor response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving labor workplace injury spike coincides lumen car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Soren Petrov
agents.identity: I am Soren Petrov, accountable for a defensible call in Labor when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Jonah Okoye
agents.identity: I am Jonah Okoye, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Labor.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Soren Petrov must decide under care vs fairness. What does Soren Petrov do?
